model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: BYU gets the nod on baseline quality: both KenPom and Torvik rate the Cougars higher overall (KenPom #23 vs. Texas #37; Torvik #33 vs. #45), and BYU’s defense grades meaningfully better than Texas’ while both offenses are high-end. Texas has a credible upset path because its offense can match BYU shot-for-shot, but over 40 minutes BYU’s stronger two-way profile and slightly higher efficiency margin make it the safer advance. The main caveat is BYU’s documented injury attrition (including Richie Saunders out for the season), which lowers confidence, but the remaining team-level metrics still favor BYU.

reasoningStep.title: West 6/11: BYU vs Texas
reasoningStep.summary: BYU is the pick because it rates better in both major efficiency systems and has the stronger defensive baseline, while Texas’ path relies more on offensive variance in a near-even scoring matchup.
evidence: KenPom: BYU #23 (AdjO 125.5, AdjD 102.3) vs Texas #37 (AdjO 125.0, AdjD 106.0).
Torvik: BYU #33 (AdjO rank 16, AdjD rank 88) vs Texas #45 (AdjO rank 21, AdjD rank 125).
Injury/availability context from web reporting: BYU has notable season-ending injuries including Richie Saunders (torn ACL) and Dawson Baker, which reduces depth and slightly increases upset risk.
Matchup logic: with similar offensive ceilings, BYU’s better defensive efficiency and higher overall adjusted margin provide a more reliable path to winning if shooting variance normalizes.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.
